title: mrmopt
text: Media Response Modeling (MRM) part broader set techniques models Media Mix Modeling (MMM) umbrella. MRM focuses modeling, quantifying, analyzing non-linear saturation (diminishing returns) relationship media spend /(impressions/clicks/GRPs) business outcomes, sales conversions. relationships modeled non-linear response curves capture diminishing returns media investments. Establishing response curves mix media critical optimizing media mix better performance, allows marketers understand effectiveness media investments beyond observed levels.

what: mrmopt: Media Response Modeling in R
title: mrmopt
text: mrmopt R package designed facilitate media response modeling using Bayesian methods. provides tools fitting media response models, analyzing response curves, optimizing media mix improved performance. goal mrmopt package make easier marketers analysts apply media response modeling techniques data, enabling make informed decisions media investments. focus package comprehensively model uncertainly around response curves build optimizations broader set scenarios, rather just optimizing single point estimate response curve. Bayesian estimation powered brms package enables simultaneously robust flexible framework modeling media response remaining within bounds set non-linear response models commonly used (Logistic, Weibull, Gompertz, etc.). flexible, package designed user-friendly accessible marketers analysts may extensive experience Bayesian modeling, providing smart automations, defaults, tools guide customization.

text: can install mrmopt package GitHub using devtools package. don’t devtools installed, can install CRAN: , can install mrmopt package GitHub:

title: mrmopt
text: Fit Bayesian media response models using brms. Analyze response curves understand media effectiveness. Optimize media mix improved performance.

title: mrmopt
text: Visit companion website detailed documentation, tutorials, examples use mrmopt package media response modeling: https://bdshaff.github.io/mrmopt/

what: Fit a response curve model using brms — fit_response
title: Fit a response curve model using brms — fit_response
text: function fits response curve model using brms package.

title: Fit a response curve model using brms — fit_response
code: fit_response(   data,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   auto = TRUE,   type = "gompertz",   scale_data = TRUE,   scale_method = "min_max",   prior = NULL,   chains = 4,   iter = 4000,   warmup = 1000,   control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95),   infer_xrange = NULL,   infer_length = 1000,   cost_per_unit = 1,   response_rate = 1,   ... )

title: Fit a response curve model using brms — fit_response
text: data data frame containing data fitted. x name independent variable (predictor). y name dependent variable (response). auto Logical indicating whether automatically scale data set priors. Default TRUE. type type response curve model fit. Options "gompertz", "logistic", "weibull", "exponential". infer_xrange Optional range x values inference. NULL, uses range x data. infer_length number points generate inference. Default 1000. cost_per_unit cost per unit independent variable. Default 1.0. response_rate response rate used return calculations. Default 1.0. ... Additional arguments passed brms::brm function. infer_scaled Logical indicating whether return scaled inference results. Default TRUE.

title: Fit a response curve model using brms — fit_response
text: fitted model object.

title: Fit a response curve model using brms — fit_response
text: function fits response curve model using specified type returns fitted model object.

what: Define a response form for a nonlinear model — hlpr_define_response_form
title: Define a response form for a nonlinear model — hlpr_define_response_form
text: function defines response form nonlinear model based specified type. uses `brms` package create formula response variable.

title: Define a response form for a nonlinear model — hlpr_define_response_form
Define a response form for a nonlinear model — hlpr_define_response_form
text: type character string specifying type response form. Valid options "logistic", "log_logistic", "gompertz", "reflected_gompertz", "weibull", "reflected_weibull". x character string representing name predictor variable. y character string representing name response variable.

title: Define a response form for a nonlinear model — hlpr_define_response_form
text: `brms` formula object representing response form.

title: Define a response form for a nonlinear model — hlpr_define_response_form
text: function supports following response forms: logistic: \(y = c + ((d - c)/(1 + exp(b*(x - e))))\) log_logistic: \(y = c + ((d - c)/(1 + exp(b*(log(x) - log(e))))\) gompertz: \(y = c + (d - c) * exp(-exp(b * (x - e)))\) reflected_gompertz: \(y = c + (d - c) * (1 - exp(-exp(b * (-x + e))))\) weibull: \(y = c + (d - c) * exp(-exp(b * (log(x) - log(e))))\) reflected_weibull: \(y = c + (d - c) * (1 - exp(-exp(b * (-log(x) + log(e))))\)

text: Get Ranges Maximum Marginal Returns Average Returns function computes ranges x values corresponding maximum marginal returns (MR) average returns (AR) list marginal response models (MRMs).

code: mrm_returns_ranges(   mrm,   xrange = NULL,   length.out = 1000,   scaled = TRUE,   cost_per_unit = 1,   response_rate = 1 )

title: Get Ranges of Maximum Marginal Returns and Average Returns This function computes the ranges of x values corresponding to the maximum marginal returns (MR) and average returns (AR) for a list of marginal response models (MRMs). — mrm_returns_ranges
text: mrm list fitted model objects (e.g., brmsfit objects). xrange numeric vector length 2 specifying range x values consider. NULL, range determined data. length.integer specifying number points generate x-axis. Default 1000. scaled logical value indicating whether use scaled values calculations. Default TRUE.

text: data frame containing channels corresponding x ranges maximum MR AR.

title: Get Ranges of Maximum Marginal Returns and Average Returns This function computes the ranges of x values corresponding to the maximum marginal returns (MR) and average returns (AR) for a list of marginal response models (MRMs). — mrm_returns_ranges

what: Generate constraints for optimization based on MRM return rates or total spend This function generates constraints for optimization based on the return rates from the MRM models or a total spend constraint. The constraints include lower bounds, upper bounds, and initial values for each channel. — opt_generate_constraints
title: Generate constraints for optimization based on MRM return rates or total spend This function generates constraints for optimization based on the return rates from the MRM models or a total spend constraint. The constraints include lower bounds, upper bounds, and initial values for each channel. — opt_generate_constraints
text: Generate constraints optimization based MRM return rates total spend function generates constraints optimization based return rates MRM models total spend constraint. constraints include lower bounds, upper bounds, initial values channel.

title: Generate constraints for optimization based on MRM return rates or total spend This function generates constraints for optimization based on the return rates from the MRM models or a total spend constraint. The constraints include lower bounds, upper bounds, and initial values for each channel. — opt_generate_constraints
code: opt_generate_constraints(   mrms_list,   type = "return_rates",   bounds_multiplier = 3,   total_x = NULL )

title: Generate constraints for optimization based on MRM return rates or total spend This function generates constraints for optimization based on the return rates from the MRM models or a total spend constraint. The constraints include lower bounds, upper bounds, and initial values for each channel. — opt_generate_constraints
text: mrms_list list MRM models, model contains return rates min-max values channels. type type use generating constraints. Options "return_rates" "total_bounded". Default "return_rates". bounds_multiplier numeric value greater 1 multiply return rate bounds type "return_rates". Default 3. total_x numeric value representing total spend constraint type "total_bounded". Must greater 0. Default NULL.

title: Generate constraints for optimization based on MRM return rates or total spend This function generates constraints for optimization based on the return rates from the MRM models or a total spend constraint. The constraints include lower bounds, upper bounds, and initial values for each channel. — opt_generate_constraints
text: data frame containing channel names, lower bounds (lb), upper bounds (ub), initial values (x0), weekly spend, total spend channel.

title: Generate constraints for optimization based on MRM return rates or total spend This function generates constraints for optimization based on the return rates from the MRM models or a total spend constraint. The constraints include lower bounds, upper bounds, and initial values for each channel. — opt_generate_constraints
text: type "return_rates", function calculates lower upper bounds channel based return rates MRM models, multiplied bounds_multiplier. initial value (x0) set midpoint lower upper bounds. type "total_bounded", function sets lower bound 0 upper bound total_x channel, initial value (x0) set total_x divided number channels.

what: Optimal Mix Function This function computes the optimal mix of channels to maximize the total response given a set of response functions and constraints. — opt_mix
title: Optimal Mix Function This function computes the optimal mix of channels to maximize the total response given a set of response functions and constraints. — opt_mix
text: Optimal Mix Function function computes optimal mix channels maximize total response given set response functions constraints.

title: Optimal Mix Function This function computes the optimal mix of channels to maximize the total response given a set of response functions and constraints. — opt_mix
code: opt_mix(   mrms,   objective = "maximize_y",   constraints_type = "return_rates",   total_x = NULL,   x0 = NULL,   lb = NULL,   ub = NULL,   ineq_constr = NULL,   xtol_rel = 1e-10,   maxeval = 1000 )

title: Optimal Mix Function This function computes the optimal mix of channels to maximize the total response given a set of response functions and constraints. — opt_mix
text: mrms list response functions, function takes numeric vector input returns numeric value. objective character string indicating objective optimization. Default "maximize_y". constraints_type character string indicating type constraints apply. Default "return_rates". total_x numeric value representing total budget constraint optimization. x0 optional numeric vector representing initial guess optimization. NULL, default value used. lb optional numeric vector representing lower bounds channel. NULL, default values used. ub optional numeric vector representing upper bounds channel. NULL, default values used. ineq_constr optional function representing additional inequality constraints. NULL, default constraints used. xtol_rel numeric value representing relative tolerance optimization algorithm. Default 1.0e-10. maxeval integer value representing maximum number evaluations optimization algorithm. Default 1000. location character string indicating location response functions. Default "center".

title: Optimal Mix Function This function computes the optimal mix of channels to maximize the total response given a set of response functions and constraints. — opt_mix
text: list containing optimization results, including optimal channel mix maximum response value.

Plot Optimal Mix — plot_optimal_mix
text: response_funs named list response functions. res result object optimization containing optimal solution.

title: Plot Optimal Mix — plot_optimal_mix
text: ggplot object showing response curves optimal points.

title: Response Curve Function — response
text: function computes response curve based specified model type. supports Gompertz model Richards model.

title: Response Curve Function — response
Response Curve Function — response
text: x numeric vector representing input values. params list containing parameters model. include: b: slope parameter (numeric). c: lower asymptote (numeric). d: upper asymptote (numeric). e: half-saturation (numeric). type character string specifying model type. can either "gompertz" "richards".

title: Response Curve Function — response
text: numeric vector representing response values computed model.

title: Compare Response Curve Models — response_models_comparison
text: function generates comparison plot various response curve models using specified parameters.

title: Compare Response Curve Models — response_models_comparison
code: response_models_comparison(   x = seq(0, 1, by = 0.01),   b = -8,   c = 0,   d = 10,   e = 0.5 )

title: Compare Response Curve Models — response_models_comparison
text: x numeric vector representing input values response curves. Default seq(0, 1, = 0.01). b numeric value representing 'b' parameter response curves. Default -8. c numeric value representing 'c' parameter response curves. Default 0. d numeric value representing 'd' parameter response curves. Default 10. e numeric value representing 'e' parameter response curves. Default 0.5.

title: Gompertz Response Model — rm_Gompertz
text: function computes Gompertz response model given vector x values. takes parameters b, c, d, e define Gompertz function.

title: Gompertz Response Model — rm_Gompertz
Gompertz Response Model — rm_Gompertz
text: x numeric vector x values. b numeric value representing steepness curve. c numeric value representing lower asymptote. d numeric value representing upper asymptote. e numeric value representing x-value sigmoid's midpoint.

title: Gompertz Response Model — rm_Gompertz
text: numeric vector length x, representing computed Gompertz response values.

title: Gompertz Response Model — rm_Gompertz
text: Gompertz function defined : $$y = c + (d - c) * exp(-exp( b * (x - e)))$$

title: Gompertz Response Model — rm_Gompertz
code: x_values <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.1) b <- -0.5 c <- 0 d <- 1 e <- 5 result <- rm_Gompertz(x_values, b, c, d, e) plot(x_values, result, type = "l", main = "Gompertz Response Model", xlab = "x", ylab = "y")

title: Reflected Gompertz Response Model — rm_GompertzRef
text: function computes reflected Gompertz response model given vector x values. takes parameters b, c, d, e define reflected Gompertz function.

title: Reflected Gompertz Response Model — rm_GompertzRef
Reflected Gompertz Response Model — rm_GompertzRef
text: x numeric vector x values. b numeric value representing steepness curve. c numeric value representing lower asymptote. d numeric value representing upper asymptote. e numeric value representing x-value sigmoid's midpoint.

title: Reflected Gompertz Response Model — rm_GompertzRef
text: numeric vector length x, representing computed reflected Gompertz response values.

title: Reflected Gompertz Response Model — rm_GompertzRef
text: reflected Gompertz function defined : $$y = c + (d - c) * (1 - exp(-exp( b * (-x + e))))$$

title: Reflected Gompertz Response Model — rm_GompertzRef
code: x_values <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.1) b <- -0.5 c <- 0 d <- 1 e <- 5 result <- rm_GompertzRef(x_values, b, c, d, e) plot(x_values, result, type = "l", main = "Reflected Gompertz Response Model", xlab = "x", ylab = "y")

title: Reflected Weibull Response Model — rm_WeibullRef
Reflected Weibull Response Model — rm_WeibullRef
text: x numeric vector x values. b numeric value representing steepness curve. c numeric value representing lower asymptote. d numeric value representing upper asymptote. e numeric value representing x-value sigmoid's midpoint.

title: Reflected Weibull Response Model — rm_WeibullRef
text: numeric vector length x, representing computed reflected Weibull response values.

title: Reflected Weibull Response Model — rm_WeibullRef
text: reflected Weibull function defined : $$y = c + (d - c) * (1 - exp(-exp( b * (-log(x) + log(e)))))$$

title: Reflected Weibull Response Model — rm_WeibullRef
code: x_values <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.1) b <- -5 c <- 0 d <- 1 e <- 5 result <- rm_WeibullRef(x_values, b, c, d, e) plot(x_values, result, type = "l", main = "Reflected Weibull Response Model", xlab = "x", ylab = "y")

title: Dispatch Response Model — rm_dispatch
text: function dispatches appropriate response model based type provided.

title: Dispatch Response Model — rm_dispatch
Dispatch Response Model — rm_dispatch
text: type string indicating type response model dispatch.

title: Dispatch Response Model — rm_dispatch
text: function corresponding specified response model.

title: Dispatch Response Model — rm_dispatch
text: function takes string input `type` returns corresponding response model function. available response models : - "logistic" - "log_logistic" - "gompertz" - "reflected_gompertz" - "weibull" - "reflected_weibull"
